what_it_does: This file is the gallery runtime core: it loads the pieces manifest and persisted feedback, drives mockup selection and version switching, renders single or side-by-side iframe panes, manages comment-pin and element-scope feedback overlays, scales frames to the viewport, and composes the adaptive work|panel viewer shell (split ratio, polarity, zoom, panel fills) for desktop and mobile.
observations: Maintains a cluster of mutable closure state (cur, curV, compare, commentMode, scopeMode, feedback, manifest, mockups, workZoom, splitCfg, polarity).
Registers as a single DNA.gallery module named stage with an init(ctx) callback and exposes ctx getters plus boot/onFrameApplied hooks.
Loads manifest.json, grown/tokens/grammar/application faces via DNA.api, and loads/saves feedback through DNA.api.feedback and DNA.api.saveFeedback.
Re-renders the entire comment overlay layer on every renderStage/fit/resize by measuring the active frame's bounding rect.
Implements scope mode by wiring same-origin mousemove/click listeners into each iframe to highlight direct children of .view/.screen/body and compute unit span addresses.
Implements comment mode by overlaying a click-catcher sized to the frame, capturing fractional coordinates, describing the element via elementFromPoint, and persisting pins per mockup+version.
fit() scales frames with CSS transform and negative margins to collapse the layout box, with separate narrow/mobile and desktop math.

Composes the work|panel viewer from application.json viewer_layout via DNA.splitRatio, DNA.splitPane, DNA.pinchZoom, and DNA.injectVars, with localStorage persistence for polarity and split proportion.
The panel fill selector re-parents existing drawer scroll containers (layouts/grammar/tokens) into the panel host instead of rebuilding them.
Exposes global window shims (boot, select, step, toggleCompare, etc.) for HTML on* attributes and rail call sites.
standouts: Intentionally keeps stage, version/compare, feedback, comments, scope, and viewer shell in one file to avoid window globals for shared mutable state.
Comment and scope overlays are rebuilt from scratch on every renderStage/fit/resize using live getBoundingClientRect measurements.
fit() uses transform:scale plus negative margins to make the scaled iframe occupy its visual size and avoid phantom scrollbars on mobile.
Panel fills are implemented by moving existing drawer DOM nodes into the panel host and back, rather than duplicating their content.
Viewer shell is data-driven from application.json, grammar, and tokens via DNA helpers (splitRatio, injectVars, splitPane, pinchZoom, control).
Compare mode disables comment and scope, and leaving the compare panel fill automatically exits compare.
conventions: IIFE module registered under global.DNA.gallery with an init(ctx) callback.
Exposes a ctx surface of getters and boot/onFrameApplied hooks rather than direct exports.
Mutable closure state shared across related functions instead of parameter passing.
HTML construction via template literals with esc() for text escaping.
Global window shims for HTML event attributes and external call sites.
Dataset attributes and CSS custom properties used to communicate shell state to CSS.
Async boot sequence with try/catch fallbacks for offline or missing API behavior.
concerns: Large shared mutable closure makes state interactions difficult to trace.
Global shims and reliance on APP/GRAM/TOKENS as implicit globals create hidden dependencies.
Same-origin iframe access for scope/comments silently degrades if pieces are cross-origin or fail to load.
fit() mixes layout math with DOM side effects and calls renderComments, which rebuilds the overlay layer.
saveFeedback uses a sequence counter to ignore stale saves but does not retry or surface persistent errors.
panelFill references window.toggleCompare and window[render] globals that may be undefined.
The 920px mobile breakpoint is repeated in multiple functions and may drift from CSS media queries.
notes: This is the gallery's central controller: it owns selection, rendering, feedback, comments, scope, and the adaptive work|panel viewer shell.
questions: What is the exact schema of manifest.json and feedback.json?
Which other DNA.gallery modules provide ctx.loadGrown, ctx.loadTokens, and ctx.loadGrammar?
Where are applyTokens, DNA.api.*, DNA.splitRatio, DNA.splitPane, DNA.pinchZoom, DNA.injectVars, DNA.surfaces, and DNA.control defined?
Is APP a global set only by loadViewer, and is it safe that buildScrubV references APP before loadViewer completes?
Which HTML elements with IDs such as stage, panes, vtabs, scrubV, cmpL, cmpR, and panelFills are provided by the host markup?
What is the intended behavior when scope or comment mode is used on a mockup whose iframe is not same-origin or lacks .view/.screen?
How does the rail module interact with select and buildList?
What is the intended fallback if DNA.api.dna('application') or the grammar/tokens calls fail?
purpose_vs_actual: The header says stage.js is the gallery's core extracted from index.html and kept as one cohesive module because the shared mutable state cluster would otherwise require window globals; the file matches that purpose, containing stage rendering, version/compare controls, feedback, comments, scope, and the viewer shell.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to centralize all gallery orchestration—piece browsing, version comparison, annotated feedback, element scoping, and an adaptive data-driven viewer shell—inside a single closure so related state stays coherent without leaking into the global object.
novelty: The file fuses an iframe-based design gallery with version comparison, same-origin element-scope addressing, fractional-coordinate comment pins, and a data-driven work|panel shell that re-parents drawer content and supports dark/light polarity, pinch zoom, and persistent split state.
contribution: This file contributes the gallery's central orchestration logic—manifest loading, mockup selection, version and compare rendering, comment/scope feedback capture, frame scaling, and the adaptive work|panel viewer shell—that a fused system would need to preserve or refactor behind explicit contracts.
summary_for_embedding: stage.js is a single IIFE gallery core module that loads the pieces manifest and feedback, drives mockup selection and version switching, renders single or compare iframe panes, captures scoped comments and element addresses, scales frames to the viewport, and composes an adaptive data-driven work|panel viewer shell with dark/light polarity and mobile interactions.
intention_for_embedding: It was intended to centralize the gallery's mutable view state and orchestration so that stage rendering, version/compare controls, feedback, comments, scope selection, and the adaptive viewer shell could be managed cohesively without leaking globals.

symbols.does: DOM helper that fetches an element by id.
symbols.description: A concise alias for document.getElementById, used throughout the module to reference UI elements by their id attributes.

symbols.does: Initializes the gallery by loading manifest, modules, feedback, and selecting the first mockup.
symbols.description: Fetches /pieces/manifest.json, loads grown/tokens/grammar modules via ctx, loads persisted feedback, builds the pieces list, selects the first mockup, and composes the work|panel viewer shell.

symbols.does: Loads persisted feedback state from DNA.api.feedback.
symbols.description: Attempts to retrieve feedback data via DNA.api.feedback; on success stores updated timestamp and mockups map, otherwise leaves feedback empty for offline/static use.

symbols.does: Ensures a feedback entry exists for a mockup with default fields.
symbols.description: Retrieves or creates the feedback object for a given mockup id, guaranteeing preferred, verdict, note, and comments keys are initialized.

symbols.does: Persists the current feedback state via DNA.api.saveFeedback.
symbols.description: Updates the updated timestamp, shows a saving indicator, calls DNA.api.saveFeedback, and updates the indicator to saved or failed while ignoring stale responses via a sequence counter.

symbols.does: Selects a mockup by index and refreshes the UI.
symbols.description: Wraps the index around the mockups array, sets current version to latest, updates title/meta, ensures rail visibility, rebuilds version tabs/scrubber/compare selects, reflects feedback controls, renders the stage, and rebuilds the skim.

symbols.does: Moves the current selection by a delta.
symbols.description: Advances or rewinds the mockup index by calling select with the offset.

symbols.does: Toggles element-scope selection mode.
symbols.description: Flips scopeMode on/off, updates the scope button, disables comment mode if active, hides the scope chip when off, and wires scope handlers to iframe documents.

symbols.does: Attaches mousemove/click handlers inside an iframe for scope selection.
symbols.description: Installs same-origin event listeners on an iframe document to highlight top-level child elements, compute unit span addresses, and display the selected element's scope chip.

symbols.does: Returns the scope root element inside an iframe document.
symbols.description: Chooses the .view or .screen element, falling back to body, as the container whose direct children are scoped.

symbols.does: Removes the highlight outline from the currently lit scope element.
symbols.description: Resets outline styles on the element stored in lit and clears the reference.

symbols.does: Sets the active version and refreshes version UI and stage.
symbols.description: Updates curV, rebuilds version tabs and the mobile scrubber, and re-renders the stage.

symbols.does: Builds desktop version tab buttons for the current mockup.
symbols.description: Creates a button per version, marks the active version, and wires click handlers to setVersion.

symbols.does: Builds the mobile scrubber's version axis for the current mockup.
symbols.description: Renders version pills in the scrubV container, optionally using DNA.control for mobile version controls when the application face is loaded.

symbols.does: Populates the compare left/right version dropdowns.
symbols.description: Fills cmpL and cmpR selects with version options and defaults to oldest versus newest.

symbols.does: Toggles side-by-side version comparison mode.
symbols.description: Switches compare state, shows/hides the compare bar, disables comment and scope modes, updates button states, and re-renders the stage with two panes.

symbols.does: Toggles comment-pin placement mode.
symbols.description: Flips commentMode on/off, disables scope mode if active, updates the comment button, and re-renders comments overlay.

symbols.does: Resolves the file path for a mockup version.
symbols.description: Finds the version entry matching the requested version number and returns its file property.

symbols.does: Returns the canvas dimensions for a mockup.
symbols.description: Reads the mockup's canvas property or returns a default 1600 by 900 size used for scaling.

symbols.does: Generates HTML for a single iframe pane with a label.
symbols.description: Returns a pane wrapper containing a panel label and an iframe whose inline onload calls applyTokens.

symbols.does: Renders the stage with one or two iframe panes.
symbols.description: Builds single or compare pane HTML, updates the open-full link to the shown version, populates version info and dials, then calls fit.

symbols.does: Returns the integer version currently shown in the stage.
symbols.description: In compare mode returns the right-hand version value; otherwise returns curV.

symbols.does: Builds or rebuilds the comment overlay on the stage.
symbols.description: Removes any existing overlay, creates a layer sized to the active frame, adds a click-catcher when arming, and renders existing pins for the shown version.

symbols.does: Closes any open pin popup and comment input box.
symbols.description: Removes the .pinpop and .cmtinput elements from the DOM.

symbols.does: Displays a popup for a comment pin.
symbols.description: Positions a popup near the pin showing element info, comment text, and a delete button wired to deleteComment.

symbols.does: Handles clicks on the frame while comment mode is armed.
symbols.description: Computes fractional coordinates, describes the element under the point, opens a temporary input marker, and commits a new comment on save.

symbols.does: Saves a new comment from the temporary input box.
symbols.description: Reads the input value, removes the box if empty, otherwise calls addComment with the captured coordinates and element.

symbols.does: Describes the iframe element at fractional coordinates.
symbols.description: Uses same-origin elementFromPoint against the mockup canvas size to build a descriptor with tag, first class, and truncated text.

symbols.does: Computes the next comment sequence number.
symbols.description: Returns one greater than the highest existing comment number for a feedback entry.

symbols.does: Adds a new comment pin to the current mockup and version.
symbols.description: Pushes a comment record with fractional coordinates, element descriptor, text, and timestamp, then saves feedback and re-renders comments.

symbols.does: Removes a comment pin by sequence number.
symbols.description: Filters out the matching comment from the current mockup's feedback entry, persists, and re-renders comments.

symbols.does: Toggles the preferred version for the current mockup.
symbols.description: Sets or clears the preferred version in feedback, updates control visuals, and saves feedback.

symbols.does: Toggles the verdict for the current mockup.
symbols.description: Sets or clears the verdict value in feedback, updates control visuals, and saves feedback.

symbols.does: Updates the current mockup's note on input with debounced save.
symbols.description: Writes the note field in feedback and schedules a debounced saveFeedback call.

symbols.does: Updates UI controls to match the current mockup's feedback.
symbols.description: Toggles preference and verdict button classes and syncs the note input value.

symbols.does: Detects viewport width at or below 920px.
symbols.description: Returns true when the max-width:920px media query matches, used for mobile layout decisions.

symbols.does: Scales frames to fit the stage and repositions comments.
symbols.description: Computes a CSS transform scale based on viewport, canvas size, compare mode, and workZoom, applies it to each frame with negative margins to collapse layout box, and calls renderComments.

symbols.does: Toggles the mobile pieces rail sheet.
symbols.description: Opens or closes the body aside and shows/hides the scrim overlay.

symbols.does: Closes the mobile pieces rail sheet.
symbols.description: Removes the open class from the aside and hides the scrim.

symbols.does: Detects viewport width at or below 920px.
symbols.description: Same query as isNarrow; used to decide whether mobile viewer shell behaviors are active.

symbols.does: Reads the warmth dial value.
symbols.description: Returns the numeric value of the sw-warmth input, defaulting to 50.

symbols.does: Loads application face, grammar, tokens, and builds viewer controls.
symbols.description: Fetches application.json, grammar, and tokens via DNA.api, initializes split configuration, builds viewer controls, panel fills, wires zoom, creates the split pane and pinch zoom, and applies the viewer.

symbols.does: Applies light or dark shell polarity.
symbols.description: In dark mode injects token-derived surface variables and sets body dataset; in light mode clears injected shell keys so surface.css shows through; updates theme-color meta.

symbols.does: Sets and persists the polarity preference.
symbols.description: Stores light or dark in localStorage under vi-polarity and applies it.

symbols.does: Toggles between light and dark polarity.
symbols.description: Switches polarity to the opposite value via setPolarity.

symbols.does: Calculates the natural work fraction for the split pane.
symbols.description: Reads the viewer split and stage dimensions, canvas size, padding, and orientation to compute the fraction of the split the work slot should occupy at fit.

symbols.does: Composes the general DNA.splitPane and DNA.pinchZoom for the gallery.
symbols.description: Initializes a reusable split pane with natural fraction, clamps, mobile activation, persistence, and a pinch-zoom handler that drives workZoom while flagging pinchActive.

symbols.does: Applies the viewer layout configuration to the DOM.
symbols.description: Sets polarity, resolves and applies the work|panel split ratio, marks full-screen states, applies orientation-based flex direction from viewer_layout.reflow, and refreshes the pane or fit.

symbols.does: Changes the split ratio preset.
symbols.description: Updates splitCfg, resets workZoom unless work-full, and applies the viewer.

symbols.does: Sets the work zoom level within bounds.
symbols.description: Clamps the requested zoom between 1 and 4, updates workZoom, and calls fit.

symbols.does: Builds the split-ratio preset buttons.
symbols.description: Renders mini bar buttons for each configured split preset, marks the active one, and wires clicks to setSplitCfg.

symbols.does: Wires zoom buttons and double-tap full-screen behavior.
symbols.description: Handles zoom in/out/reset clicks and double-tap on the stage to toggle primary full-screen via the split pane.

symbols.does: Moves re-parented drawer content back to its original drawer.
symbols.description: Returns any layout, grammar, or tokens scroll container currently hosted in the panel back to its drawer element.

symbols.does: Builds the panel fill selector chips.
symbols.description: Reads viewer_layout panel accepts list and face list, generates chip buttons for versions, compare, and faces, wires panelFill, and sets up overflow fade detection.

symbols.does: Capitalizes the first letter of a string.
symbols.description: Simple helper used to label panel fill chips.

symbols.does: Updates the panel fills overflow fade indicator.
symbols.description: Toggles the is-scrollable class when there is more content to scroll to the right.

symbols.does: Switches the panel to a selected fill.
symbols.description: Updates active chip, restores drawer content, exits compare if leaving it, and either shows versions, toggles compare, or re-parents a face drawer's scroll container into the panel host.
